meaning: adj.快活的,高兴的,愉快的,[宗](主神)朱庇特的
examples: Father Whittaker appeared to be in a jovial mood.
The word came to be associated both with a loud sound and with danger! When someone speaks of a "jovial mood" or a "herculean effort," he or she is using words with origins in mythology.
Judson was a jovial, smooth-spoken man.
The agents are a jovial lot, and spend a fair amount of time laughing and telling jokes.
He is also remembered for his role as the jovial Dominic Santini in the 1980s series Airwolf.
